title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to initiate the process. During this period, you are required to complete a self-assessment form, reflecting on your achievements, challenges, and areas for growth over the past year. The self-assessment must be submitted by the end of January. Managers will then review your self-assessment and provide their feedback in February. The final performance reviews will be discussed in one-on-one meetings between employees and managers in March. To effectively complete your self-assessment, consider using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to articulate your contributions. Additionally, gather feedback from peers and reflect on your goals set in the previous review. For any questions or assistance, please reach out to the HR department at hr@company.com.
